summary: Problem 4 seeks a finite-free-probability analog of the Stam/Blachman-Stam Fisher information inequality, proving subharmonicity (superadditivity of the reciprocal) of 1/Phi_n under finite free convolution. While 40 of 45 issues are resolved and key lemmas (Hessian identity, heat-flow commutativity, Walsh normalization) are now rigorous, the proof remains incomplete due to unresolved gaps in the limiting/reduction argument and an unproven equivalence between the two definitions of finite free convolution. The 16% solvability score and the 'Best Proof' showing 100 open issues indicate no complete validated proof currently exists.
problems: Unproven equivalence (open, critical): the bilinear coefficient formula c_k = sum_{i+j=k}... has not been shown to equal the eq:walsh averaging formula, undermining the foundational definition the whole proof relies on.
Gap 3 (open): simple-rootedness of T_eps^2(p boxplus_n q) is not verified in the reduction, so the simple-roots-to-general-case limiting argument is not fully justified for the convolved polynomial.

Gap 4 (open): inversion of the lower-semicontinuity (LSC) inequality in Step C fails to handle the K=0 boundary case, leaving the reciprocal/superadditivity conclusion unestablished in a degenerate regime.
Variance additivity (open, significant): Prop. prop:props(i), Var(gamma)=Var(alpha)+Var(beta) under Psi, is incomplete — a required structural property for the inequality framework.
Aggregate signal: the recorded best proof still carries 100 open issues, indicating the current write-up has not converged to a verified state despite many local resolutions.
highlights: Strong resolution rate: 40 of 45 issues resolved, including several deep technical fixes.

Core analytic machinery is now rigorous: Hessian identity (Lemma 3, JJ* vs J*J dimensional error fixed and derivation expanded), heat-flow commutativity with the differential operator fully proved, and the missing 1/n! Walsh normalization corrected.
Lemma 1 (heat flow score derivative) survived multiple critic challenges on a factor-of-2 discrepancy and was confirmed correct via analysis and numerical verification.
Commutativity claim in Lemma 2(iii) was upgraded from a one-line citation to a complete self-contained proof, strengthening the logical backbone.
Limiting-argument direction concerns (backward inequality in §2) were analyzed and defended, isolating the remaining gaps to specific verifications (Gaps 3 and 4) rather than the overall strategy.

suggested_todos.title: Prove equivalence of the bilinear coefficient formula and the eq:walsh averaging formula — this is foundational and blocks the entire proof's definitional basis
Verify simple-rootedness of T_eps^2(p boxplus_n q) (Gap 3) to close the reduction from simple roots to the general case for the convolved polynomial
Handle the K=0 degenerate case in the Step C LSC inequality inversion (Gap 4) so the reciprocal superadditivity holds at boundary configurations
Complete the variance additivity proof for Prop. prop:props(i) under the Psi map
Reconcile the 'best proof' 100-open-issues status with the locally resolved issues by re-running validation on a consolidated proof draft to identify which gaps are genuinely still open
Assemble a single clean end-to-end proof draft integrating all resolved lemmas, then submit for a full critic pass to confirm convergence
